explanations.3: This sets a file path, but uses a default value 'node' if the OS_USERNAME variable isn't already set — the ${VAR:-default} syntax means 'use VAR, or if it's empty, use default instead'.
explanations.6: The -qxF flags mean: be quiet (no output), match the exact whole line (-x), and treat the search term as a fixed string not a pattern (-F), so it won't break if the marker contains special characters.
explanations.7: The <<EOF syntax is a 'here document' — it lets you write multiple lines of text that will all be appended to the file at once, ending when you type EOF on its own line.
explanations.10: Even though this is inside the here-document, the single quotes prevent bash from expanding ${BLOG_HOST_DIR} right now — it will stay as literal text and only get expanded later when someone sources the bashrc file.
explanations.20: The \e[33m and \e[0m are ANSI color codes — 33m makes text yellow, and 0m resets it back to normal, so 'version' appears in yellow in the terminal.
